pattern: Each element needs at most 1 operation: if it is already divisible by 3 it costs 0, otherwise it costs 1 (add or subtract 1 to reach the nearest multiple of 3).
hints: Check each element's remainder when divided by 3. A remainder of 0 means no operation needed.
A remainder of 1 means subtracting 1 (cost 1) makes it divisible. A remainder of 2 means adding 1 (cost 1) makes it divisible. Either way the cost is 1 for non-zero remainders.
Sum the indicators: answer = count of elements where num % 3 != 0.

edge_inputs.description: value 1: subtract to 0 — valid since problem allows it

stress.description: random arrays length up to 50 values up to 50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, size) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
